[Cursor IDE · Pull Request #159 · specstoryai/getspecstory](https://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/pull/159)\n\nSynthesis:\nSpecStory integrates with Cursor by automatically saving every Cursor AI conversation to Markdown inside your open project at.specstory/history/ (a timestamped file per session); nothing is sent anywhere unless you explicitly share/sync. Install from inside Cursor (Extensions marketplace → search “SpecStory” → Install). Verify: start a Cursor AI chat, then check for the.specstory/history/ directory. You can also manually export via the command palette: “SpecStory: Save AI Chat History” and share via “SpecStory: Share AI Chat History.” [1][2]\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eCursor\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://docs.specstory.com/integrations/cursor\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eSpecStory integrates seamlessly with Cursor. Cursor already helps developers move faster with inline edits, multi-line completions, and codebase chat. SpecStory adds the missing layer by capturing why the changes were made.\n[...]\nWith SpecStory enabled, every AI conversation in Cursor is auto-saved as Markdown in your project. Your reasoning, tradeoffs, and design decisions live alongside your code: versioned, reusable, and shareable.\n[...]\n- Start a chat with Cursor’s AI.\n- Open your project folder.\n- You should now see a new&#96;.specstory/history/&#96; directory.\n- Inside, you should see a Markdown file of your session, named with a timestamp.\n[...]\n- Auto-save: Every AI conversations in Cursor are automatically stored in&#96;.specstory/history/&#96; as clean Markdown.\n- Manual saves: From the Command Palette, run&#96;SpecStory: Save AI Chat History&#96; to cherry-pick and combine conversations.\n- Share conversations with a URL: Use&#96;SpecStory: Share AI Chat History&#96; to generate a private, anonymous link at&#96;share.specstory.com&#96;.\n\n- Derived rules for AI: SpecStory can extract project-level rules and custom instructions from your chats, feeding them back into Cursor to improve suggestions.\n[...]\nTool coverage includes Cursor tool v2 handlers (edit/read/terminal), plus Read/Write, Apply Patch, Grep, Glob, Ripgrep, Read Lints, and MultiEdit in Markdown exports.\n[...]\n- Commit your history: Include&#96;.specstory/history/&#96; in your repo so teammates can review reasoning in PRs.\n- Use manual saves before big changes: Preserve the conversation context before accepting multi-line or agent-driven edits.\n- Leverage derived rules: Cursor’s Project Rules evolve automatically when SpecStory is active.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eCursor - SpecStory\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://specstory.mintlify.app/integrations/cursor\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eSpecStory integrates seamlessly with Cursor. Cursor already helps developers move faster with inline edits, multi-line completions, and codebase chat. SpecStory adds the missing layer by capturing why the changes were made.\n[...]\nWith SpecStory enabled, every AI conversation in Cursor is auto-saved as Markdown in your project. Your reasoning, tradeoffs, and design decisions live alongside your code: versioned, reusable, and shareable.\n[...]\nCtrl/Cmd\n[...]\n- Open the Command Palette (&#96;Ctrl\n[...]\nCmd+Shift+\n[...]\n- Start a chat with Cursor’s AI.\n- Open your project folder.\n- You should now see a new &#96;.specstory/history/&#96; directory.\n- Inside, you should see a Markdown file of your session, named with a timestamp.\n[...]\n## How SpecStory Enhances Cursor\n[...]\n- Auto-save: Every AI conversations in Cursor are automatically stored in &#96;.specstory/history/&#96; as clean Markdown.\n- Manual saves: From the Command Palette, run &#96;SpecStory: Save AI Chat History&#96; to cherry-pick and combine conversations.\n- Share conversations with a URL: Use &#96;SpecStory: Share AI Chat History&#96; to generate a private, anonymous link at &#96;share.specstory.com&#96;.\n\n- Derived rules for AI: SpecStory can extract project-level rules and custom instructions from your chats, feeding them back into Cursor to improve suggestions.\n[...]\n- Commit your history: Include &#96;.specstory/history/&#96; in your repo so teammates can review reasoning in PRs.\n- Use manual saves before big changes: Preserve the conversation context before accepting multi-line or agent-driven edits.\n- Leverage derived rules: Cursor’s Project Rules evolve automatically when SpecStory is active.\n[...]\n- Extension mismatch: If VS Code shows SpecStory installed but Cursor doesn’t, uninstall and reinstall from Cursor’s built-in Marketplace\n[...]\n- No &#96;.specstory&#96; folder: Make sure you have an active project folder open. SpecStory only saves inside projects.\n[...]\nissue on GitHub or contact support@specstory.com\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003especstoryai/getspecstory\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eCursor IDE         ┐\n[...]\nCLI          ├──────►  .specstory/history/  ──────►  cloud.specstory.com\n[...]\n1. **Capture** - Extensions save every AI interaction locally to &#96;.specstory/history/&#96;\n[...]\n2. **Process** - Use Agent Skills to generate summaries, stats, and insights from your history\n[...]\nsessions sync to cloud\n[...]\nacross all projects in cloud\n[...]\n5. **Share** - Export\n[...]\nSpecStory integrates seamlessly with your favorite AI coding tools, automatically saving all conversations locally to &#96;.specstory/history/&#96; in your project. **Everything is local-first** - your data stays on your machine unless you choose to sync to the cloud.\n[...]\n| Product | Type | Source | Supported Agent | Min Version | Installation | Changelog |\n[...]\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n[...]\n| **Cursor Extension** | IDE | Closed | Cursor AI | v0.43.6+ | Search \"SpecStory\" in Extensions (Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+X) → Install | 📋 View |\n[...]\nGitHub Copilot | v1.300.0+ | Search \"SpecStory\" in Extensions (Cmd\n[...]\nCtrl+Shift+X) → Install | 📋 View |\n[...]\nAll sessions automatically save to &#96;.specstory/history/&#96; in your current project.\n[...]\nSessions | Past Sessions |\n[...]\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n[...]\n| **SpecStory CLI** | &#96;specstory login&#96; | Auto-pushed when using &#96;specstory run&#96; while logged in | Use &#96;specstory sync&#96; to push existing local sessions |\n[...]\n| **Cursor Extension** | Command Palette → \"SpecStory: Open Cloud Sync Configuration\" | Configure auto-sync in settings | Use sync command from Command Palette |\n[...]\n| **VSCode Extension** | Command Palette → \"SpecStory: Open Cloud Sync Configuration\" | Configure auto-sync in settings | Use sync command from Command Palette |\n[...]\n\u003e [!IMPORTANT]\n[...]\n\u003e **Local-First \u0026 Private by Default**: All sessions are saved locally to &#96;.specstory/history/&#96;. Nothing is ever sent to the cloud unless you explicitly login with. Even after logging in, you can control what gets synced.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eInstall our extensions - SpecStory\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://specstory.mintlify.app/specstory/introduction\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e\u003e Save, search and share your AI chat history with SpecStory.\n[...]\nThe SpecStory Extension (changelog) supports both Cursor and GitHub Copilot. For terminal agents like Claude Code, Cursor CLI, and Codex CLI, we offer a CLI wrapper (changelog) that captures your sessions.\n[...]\nSpecStory saves, organizes, and makes all your AI chats searchable in structured Markdown. Never lose a great solution, easily share interactions, and use conversation history to refine Copilot and Cursor with custom instructions and project rules.\n[...]\n### Extensions for VSCode \u0026 Cursor\n[...]\nOur VSIX extension works with both VSCode (with GitHub Copilot) and Cursor. Install directly from the Extension Marketplace in your preferred editor.\n[...]\n1. Open Cursor.\n2. Search for SpecStory in the Extensions Marketplace (Ctrl/CMD-Shift-X).\n3. Click Install. Consult our Changelog to make sure you're on the latest version.\n4. Once installed, SpecStory automatically saves your Cursor chats to the &#96;.specstory&#96; folder in your project.\n[...]\nThe SpecStory extension's VSIX works seamlessly with both GitHub Copilot and Cursor. You'll need to install it separately in each environment.\n[...]\nThe SpecStory CLI auto-generates markdown files from your chat sessions with terminal agents (e.g. Claude Code, Cursor CLI, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, etc.). It runs the terminal agent while saving your conversations to &#96;.specstory/history/&#96; in your project.\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Run Claude Code through SpecStory wrapper\n[...]\n# Run Cursor CLI through SpecStory wrapper\nspecstory run cursor\n[...]\n# Or sync existing sessions to markdown\nspecstory sync\n[...]\n# Run Cursor CLI through SpecStory wrapper\nspecstory run cursor\n[...]\n- Auto-save your conversations into digestible Markdown format (readable by both humans AND machines).\n- Make your vibe-coding adventures shareable via URLs at share.specstory.com.\n- Auto-magically derive Copilot custom instructions and Cursor project rules to improve agent-first coding performance.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eCursor IDE · Pull Request #159 · specstoryai/getspecstory\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/pull/159\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eSupport to export Cursor AI chat history\n[...]\n\u003e **Review (commented):**\n\u003e \n\u003e ## Pull request overview\n\u003e \n\u003e This PR adds support for exporting Cursor IDE chat history by implementing a new &#96;cursoride&#96; provider that reads directly from Cursor's SQLite databases.\n\u003e \n\u003e **Changes:**\n\u003e \n\u003e - Implements a new provider for Cursor IDE that reads from global and workspace-specific SQLite databases\n\u003e - Adds workspace matching logic to filter conversations by project path\n\u003e - Includes comprehensive tool invocation handlers for various Cursor IDE tools (file operations, grep, shell commands)\n\u003e \n\u003e ### Reviewed changes\n\u003e \n\u003e Copilot reviewed 16 out of 16 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.\n\u003e \n\u003e **Show a summary per file**\n\u003e \n\u003e | File | Description |\n\u003e | --- | --- |\n\u003e | specstory-cli/pkg/spi/factory/registry.go | Registers the new cursoride provider |\n[...]\n\u003e | specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursoride/provider.go | Main provider implementation with Check, DetectAgent, GetAgentChatSessions, and WatchAgent methods |\n[...]\n\u003e | specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursoride/database.go | SQLite database operations for reading composer and bubble data |\n[...]\n\u003e | specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursoride/workspace.go | Workspace matching logic to filter conversations by project path |\n[...]\n\u003e | specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursoride/watcher.go | File watching implementation with throttling and safety net polling |\n[...]\n\u003e | specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursoride/agent_session.go | Conversion from Cursor composer data to unified AgentChatSession format |\n[...]\n\u003e | specstory\n[...]\n\u003e | specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursoride/tool_read_file.go | Handler for read_file tool invocations |\n[...]\n\u003e | specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursoride/tool_shell.go | Handler for terminal\n[...]\n\u003e | specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursoride/path_utils.go | OS-specific path resolution for Cursor IDE databases |\n[...]\n\u003e | specstory-cli/main.go | Adds cursoride-db-check-interval flag for watch command |\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",
      "title": "Web search results"
    }

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Cursor cannot directly read .specstory files; it requires the conversation history to be imported into its interface or converted into a compatible format for processing.</details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="web_fetch">

Result:

```json
{
  "markdown": "Cursor\n\nIntegrations›Cursor\n\nSpecStory integrates seamlessly with [Cursor](https://cursor.com/). Cursor already helps developers move faster with inline edits, multi-line completions, and codebase chat. SpecStory adds the missing layer by capturing why the changes were made.\n\nWith SpecStory enabled, every AI conversation in Cursor is auto-saved as Markdown in your project. Your reasoning, tradeoffs, and design decisions live alongside your code: versioned, reusable, and shareable.\n\n## Install the SpecStory Extension\n\nVerify installation:\n\n- Open the Command Palette (&#96;Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P&#96;).\n- Type&#96;SpecStory&#96;, the commands should appear.\n\nWarning\n\nIf you install SpecStory directly from the [Visual Studio Marketplace](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=SpecStory.specstory-vscode) website, you may end up with VS Code having the extension but not Cursor. Always install directly from inside Cursor to avoid this issue.\n\n## Verify It Works\n\n- Start a chat with Cursor’s AI.\n- Open your project folder.\n- You should now see a new&#96;.specstory/history/&#96; directory.\n- Inside, you should see a Markdown file of your session, named with a timestamp.\n\nTip\n\nSpecStory is local-first by default. Nothing leaves your machine unless you explicitly share it.\n\n## How SpecStory Enhances Cursor\n\n- Auto-save: Every AI conversations in Cursor are automatically stored in&#96;.specstory/history/&#96; as clean Markdown.\n- Manual saves: From the Command Palette, run&#96;SpecStory: Save AI Chat History&#96; to cherry-pick and combine conversations.\n- Share conversations with a URL: Use&#96;SpecStory: Share AI Chat History&#96; to generate a private, anonymous link at&#96;share.specstory.com&#96;.\n\n- Derived rules for AI: SpecStory can extract project-level rules and custom instructions from your chats, feeding them back into Cursor to improve suggestions.\n\nInfo\n\nRule derivation requires:\n\n- Auto-save enabled.\n- Signed-in SpecStory extension.\n\nInfo\n\nTool coverage includes Cursor tool v2 handlers (edit/read/terminal), plus Read/Write, Apply Patch, Grep, Glob, Ripgrep, Read Lints, and MultiEdit in Markdown exports.\n\n## Best Practices for Cursor Users\n\n- Commit your history: Include&#96;.specstory/history/&#96; in your repo so teammates can review reasoning in PRs.\n- Use manual saves before big changes: Preserve the conversation context before accepting multi-line or agent-driven edits.\n- Leverage derived rules: Cursor’s [Project Rules](https://docs.cursor.com/context/rules-for-ai#project-rules-recommended) evolve automatically when SpecStory is active.\n\n## Why This Matters\n\nCursor accelerates what changes in your codebase. SpecStory ensures you never lose why those changes happened. Together, they give you:\n\n- Faster debugging and onboarding\n- Traceable architectural decisions\n- Cleaner collaboration on AI-assisted code\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n- Extension mismatch: If VS Code shows SpecStory installed but Cursor doesn’t, uninstall and reinstall from Cursor’s built-in Marketplace.\n- No&#96;.specstory&#96; folder: Make sure you have an active project folder open. SpecStory only saves inside projects.\n- Still stuck? File an issue on [GitHub](https://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/issues) or contact [support@specstory.com](mailto:support@specstory.com).",
  "url": "https://docs.specstory.com/integrations/cursor"
}
```
User decision: **accepted**

{
  "isFinal": true,
  "references": [
    {
      "chunk": "Links:\n1. [SpecStory: Project Memory for AI Coding Assistants](https://www.logique.co.id/blog/en/2026/01/20/specstory-memory/)\n2. [Quickstart - SpecStory](https://specstory.mintlify.app/specstory/quickstart)\n3. [Cursor - SpecStory](https://specstory.mintlify.app/integrations/cursor)\n4. [Features](https://docs.specstory.com/specstory/features)\n5. [Keep Cursor Aligned with Your Intent | SpecStory Blog](https://specstory.com/blog/keeping-cursor-aligned-with-your-intent)\n\nSynthesis:\nSpecStory records every Cursor AI session into.specstory/history/ as separate Markdown files, and those history files are not automatically used as active instructions. If you enable derived rules (specstory.derivedRules.enabled / derivedCursorRules) and have autosave on plus signed-in SpecStory, SpecStory extracts “rule-like” decisions from your saved conversations and updates Cursor’s rule files automatically:.cursor/rules/derived-rules.mdc (or derived-cursor-rules.mdc) so Cursor can apply them, and (optionally) Copilot instructions via.github/copilot-instructions.md [1][2][3][4].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eSpecStory: Project Memory for AI Coding Assistants\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.logique.co.id/blog/en/2026/01/20/specstory-memory/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e- Automatically saves each AI coding session—prompts, responses, diffs, and code blocks—as Markdown files inside your project.\n- Lets you curate, merge, annotate, and share conversations as public URLs for teammates or reviewers.\n- Can derive AI rules from those conversations and keep Cursor and GitHub Copilot aligned with your evolving architectural and coding decisions.\n[...]\nEvery AI interaction is automatically saved as a separate&#96;.md&#96; file under:\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n.specstory/history/\n[...]\n- These Markdown history files do not automatically influence Cursor or GitHub Copilot.\n- They are documentation and context, not active instructions.\n[...]\n- Skimming a relevant history file and pasting key constraints into a new prompt.\n- Using Cursor’s&#96;@&#96; reference feature to pull specific history files into a chat session.\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\"specstory.derivedRules.enabled\": true\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[...]\nSpecStory analyzes your conversations and maintains live rule files for your AI assistants:\n[...]\n- &#96;.cursor/rules/derived-rules.mdc&#96; for Cursor\n- &#96;.github/copilot-instructions.md&#96; for GitHub Copilot\n[...]\nans conversations for rule-like statements\n[...]\nCursor already treats&#96;.cursor/rules/*.mdc&#96; as first-class guidance for both autocomplete and chat.\n[...]\n1. You work as usual; chats and code diffs are auto-saved.\n2. SpecStory stores them in&#96;.specstory/history/&#96;.\n3. With derived rules enabled, those chats update&#96;.cursor/rules/derived-rules.mdc&#96;.\n4. Cursor uses those rules to guide autocomplete and chat.\n[...]\nOlder versions of rules are backed up automatically, so you can inspect or roll back changes if needed.\n[...]\n| Situation | History (.md) | Derived Rules |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| Understand past decisions | ✅ Ideal | ❌ Not needed |\n| One prompt needs extra context | ✅ Reference or paste | Optional |\n| All suggestions should stay consistent | ❌ Not enough | ✅ Essential |\n| Architecture or standards change | ✅ Document it | ✅ Auto-updated |\n[...]\nand GitHub Cop\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eQuickstart - SpecStory\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://specstory.mintlify.app/specstory/quickstart\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eWhen you install SpecStory, it runs quietly in the background and saves your AI chat history for the active project inside &#96;.specstory/history/&#96;. You can toggle autosave at any time in settings.\n[...]\n## Derive rules for AI automatically\n[...]\nRule derivation launched in SpecStory v0.7.0 for Cursor and expanded to GitHub Copilot in &#96;v0.11.0&#96;. To enable it, turn on autosave and sign in to the SpecStory extension.\n[...]\n- GitHub Copilot custom instructions\n- Cursor project rules or Cursor101.com\n[...]\nSpecStory analyzes your conversations to capture:\n[...]\n- The level of detail you typically request from AI responses.\n- Any rule or header structure you rely on when guiding the model.\n- How you iterate between suggestions and code edits.\n- Architectural patterns that repeatedly surface in your prompts.\n[...]\nThe extension then writes out editor-specific rules in the appropriate locations:\n[...]\n1. &#96;.github/copilot-instructions.md&#96;\n2. &#96;.cursor/rules/derived-rules.mdc&#96;\n[...]\n### Prompt headers\n[...]\nSpecStory relies on consistent headers to generate high-quality rules. We include a default template informed by community best practices.\n[...]\n- Spend less time re-explaining preferences in every prompt.\n- Get faster, more aligned suggestions from Copilot and Cursor.\n- Let project rules evolve automatically as conversations provide new guidance.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eCursor - SpecStory\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://specstory.mintlify.app/integrations/cursor\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eSpecStory integrates seamlessly with Cursor. Cursor already helps developers move faster with inline edits, multi-line completions, and codebase chat. SpecStory adds the missing layer by capturing why the changes were made.\n[...]\nWith SpecStory enabled, every AI conversation in Cursor is auto-saved as Markdown in your project. Your reasoning, tradeoffs, and design decisions live alongside your code: versioned, reusable, and shareable.\n[...]\n- Start a chat with Cursor’s AI.\n- Open your project folder.\n- You should now see a new &#96;.specstory/history/&#96; directory.\n- Inside, you should see a Markdown file of your session, named with a timestamp.\n[...]\n- Auto-save: Every AI conversations in Cursor are automatically stored in &#96;.specstory/history/&#96; as clean Markdown.\n- Manual saves: From the Command Palette, run &#96;SpecStory: Save AI Chat History&#96; to cherry-pick and combine conversations.\n- Share conversations with a URL: Use &#96;SpecStory: Share AI Chat History&#96; to generate a private, anonymous link at &#96;share.specstory.com&#96;.\n\n- Derived rules for AI: SpecStory can extract project-level rules and custom instructions from your chats, feeding them back into Cursor to improve suggestions.\n[...]\nRule derivation requires:\n[...]\n- Auto-save enabled.\n- Signed-in SpecStory extension.\n[...]\n- Commit your history: Include &#96;.specstory/history/&#96; in your repo so teammates can review reasoning in PRs.\n- Use manual saves before big changes: Preserve the conversation context before accepting multi-line or agent-driven edits.\n- Leverage derived rules: Cursor’s Project Rules evolve automatically when SpecStory is active.\n[...]\n- Extension mismatch: If VS Code shows SpecStory installed but Cursor doesn’t, uninstall and reinstall from Cursor’s built-in Marketplace.\n- No &#96;.specstory&#96; folder: Make sure you have an active project folder open. SpecStory only saves inside projects.\n- Still stuck? File an issue on GitHub or contact support@specstory.com.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eFeatures\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://docs.specstory.com/specstory/features\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eSpecStory automatically saves chat history in your project's&#96;.specstory&#96; folder. Each conversation is stored as a separate Markdown file so you retain the full context of every interaction.\n[...]\n### Derive rules for AI automatically\n[...]\nTurn on automatic rule derivation with the&#96;specstory.derivedRules&#96; setting (available in SpecStory v0.7.0 and later).\n[...]\nTo use this feature, enable autosave and sign in to the SpecStory extension. Authentication prevents abuse because each derivation calls an LLM.\n[...]\nKey capabilities:\n[...]\n- Generate&#96;derived-rules.mdc&#96; in&#96;.cursor/rules&#96; and&#96;copilot-instructions.md&#96; in&#96;.github/&#96;.\n- Back up previous versions in&#96;.specstory/ai_rules_backups&#96; and add the folder to&#96;.gitignore&#96; automatically.\n- Customize headers used in the generated rule files through settings.\n[...]\n### System prompt used for rule derivation\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nYou are an expert software engineer responsible for maintaining a \"living\" and \"evolving\" AI coding assistant rules file. This file defines all project rules, coding standards, workflow guidelines, references, documentation structures, and best practices. Each time you receive new user–AI interactions, you must merge any relevant new guidelines, clarifications, or decisions into the file which is stored in the root of the project and is obeyed by the AI coding assistant.\n[...]\n5. If version updates or references to version changes arise record them under the relevant section(s). For example, if it impacts the \"TECH STACK\" or \"PROJECT DOCUMENTATION \u0026 CONTEXT SYSTEM\", note it there.\n[...]\nTASK: Please merge\n[...]\nnew interactions into the AI coding assistant rules file as specified, and return the complete updated file content\n[...]\nreturn the updated AI coding assistant rules file as a single cohesive markdown document. Do not\n[...]\nextraneous commentary.\n[...]\nYou must return the response inside a \u003crules-file\u003e tag. Example:\n[...]\nTHING is that you\n[...]\nreturn the response inside a \u003crules-file\u003e tag\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eKeep Cursor Aligned with Your Intent | SpecStory Blog\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://specstory.com/blog/keeping-cursor-aligned-with-your-intent\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eIn our latest release of SpecStory v0.7.0, we've introduced an experimental feature that automatically derives your Cursor Project Rules.\n[...]\nOur new feature automatically creates and maintains a&#96;derived-cursor-rules.mdc&#96; file in your&#96;.cursor/rules directory&#96;. The magic happens in the background as you naturally interact with Cursor day in and day out - no special workflow required.\n[...]\nAutomatic rule generation based on your interactions with Cursor\n[...]\nVersion control friendly with automatic backup of previous versions\n[...]\nCustomizable file headers to fine-tune rule generation\n[...]\nSeamless integration with your existing workflow\n[...]\n- Have Auto-save enabled.\n[...]\n- Create an account and sign-in to the SpecStory extension.\n[...]\nOur approach is informed by extensive research on how developers effectively leverage .cursorrules. Our co-founder Greg recently published some fascinating insights on Reddit after analyzing thousands of posts about Cursor and other AI code editors.\n[...]\nProject-specific context about your tech stack and architecture\n[...]\nUpdate to SpecStory v0.7.0 or later\n[...]\nGo to Settings and enable&#96;specstory.derivedCursorRules&#96;\n[...]\nMake sure auto-save is enabled\n[...]\nSign in to your SpecStory account\n[...]\nThe system will automatically begin learning from your interactions and building your customized rules file.\n[...]\nIf you're new to Cursor Rules or want to deepen your understanding, check out Cursor's official documentation on the subject.\n[...]\nThe new Project Rules system offers incredible flexibility with path-specific configurations, semantic descriptions, and file pattern matching - all of which our generator takes full advantage of.\n[...]\nAs development teams increasingly include both human and AI contributors, maintaining alignment becomes critical. The automatically derived Cursor Rules act as a living bridge between human intent and AI assistance - evolving naturally as your project grows.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I now have a comprehensive understanding.

Let me check for Cursor rules or documentation related to this topic and read more about the .cursorindexingignore comment, which is highly relevant.

Key points for the user:

1. **.specstory is for SAVING history, not automatic reading** - SpecStory saves conversations TO .specstory/history/, but these files don't automatically become Cursor context.

2. **How to MAKE Cursor read them:**
   - **@ references**: Use `@` in chat to reference specific `.specstory/history/*.md` files.</details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

`.specstory` 主要是**保存** Cursor 对话历史，不是让 Cursor **自动**读取全部历史。要让 Cursor 用到这些内容，需要配合 SpecStory 扩展和显式引用。下面按你的项目现状说明。

## 先澄清：`.specstory` 做什么

SpecStory 会把 Cursor 里的 AI 对话自动写成 Markdown，存到：

```
.specstory/history/
```

你的项目里已经有历史文件，例如：

`2026-05-19_12-50-55Z-website-design-for-beijing-dachuan-technology.md`

**重要**：这些 `.md` 文件默认**不会**自动进入 Cursor 的上下文，它们更像项目内的“对话档案”。

---

## 第一步：确保 SpecStory 在写入历史

1. 在 **Cursor 内**安装 SpecStory 扩展（`Ctrl+Shift+X` → 搜 “SpecStory”）
   - 不要只在 VS Code 里装，否则 Cursor 可能用不了
2. 打开项目根目录（你已有 `.specstory/.project.json`，说明已关联）
3. 在 Cursor 里发起一次 AI 对话，确认 `.specstory/history/` 有新文件

常用命令（命令面板 `Ctrl+Shift+P`）：

| 命令 | 作用 |
|------|------|
| `SpecStory: Save AI Chat History` | 手动保存/合并指定对话 |
| `SpecStory: Open Cloud Sync Configuration` | 云同步配置 |

你项目里已有：

```json
"specstory.cloudSync.enabled": "always"
```

说明云同步是开着的。

---

## 第二步：让 Cursor **读取**历史内容（3 种方式）

### 方式 1：用 `@` 显式引用（最直接）

在 Cursor Chat / Agent 输入框里：

```
@.specstory/history/2026-05-19_12-50-55Z-website-design-for-beijing-dachuan-technology.md
```

或输入 `@` 后浏览到 `.specstory/history/` 下的文件。

你的 `.cursorindexingignore` 已写明设计意图：

```2:3:f:\4 Code\web-veloxisai\.cursorindexingignore
# Don't index SpecStory auto-save files, but allow explicit context inclusion via @ references
.specstory/**
```

含义是：

- `.specstory/**` **不参与**代码库索引（避免污染 codebase search）
- 但通过 **`@` 引用** 仍可作为上下文传给 AI

适合：续接某次设计讨论、回顾“为什么这样改”。

---

### 方式 2：开启 Derived Rules（间接、持续生效）

若希望历史对话里的**偏好、架构决策**自动影响后续 Cursor 行为，可开 SpecStory 的 **规则推导**：

1. 开启 SpecStory 自动保存
2. 登录 SpecStory 账号
3. 在设置里启用（名称可能随版本略有不同）：
   - `specstory.derivedRules.enabled`，或
   - `specstory.derivedCursorRules`

开启后，SpecStory 会从对话中提取规则，写入例如：

```
.cursor/rules/derived-rules.mdc
```

Cursor 会自动读取 `.cursor/rules/` 下的规则，后续 Chat / Agent 都会受影响。

**注意**：这是把对话**提炼成规则**，不是把整段历史原文都塞进去。

你项目里目前**还没有** `.cursor/rules/` 目录，说明 Derived Rules 可能还没启用或未生成。

---

### 方式 3：手动整理进 Cursor Rules

若某次历史对话里有长期有效的决策（配色、布局、命名规范等），可手动写进：

```
.cursor/rules/你的规则名.mdc
```

Cursor 会持久读取，比每次 `@` 历史文件更稳定。

---

## 推荐工作流

| 场景 | 做法 |
|------|------|
| 继续上次具体任务 | `@` 引用对应 `.specstory/history/*.md` |
| 让 AI 长期遵守项目约定 | 开 Derived Rules，或手写 `.cursor/rules/` |
| 大改前保留上下文 | `SpecStory: Save AI Chat History` 手动保存 |
| 团队共享决策过程 | 把 `.specstory/history/` 提交到 Git |

---

## 常见误区

1. **拼写**：目录名是 `.specstory`，不是 `.spectory`
2. **不能指望 Cursor 自动扫全部历史**：SpecStory 负责存档，Cursor 不会默认加载所有历史文件
3. **索引 vs 上下文**：`.cursorindexingignore` 排除索引 ≠ 不能用 `@` 引用
4. **Cursor 内置 transcript**：系统还会在 `%USERPROFILE%\.cursor\projects\...\agent-transcripts\` 存 transcript，那是 Cursor 自己的记录，和 `.specstory` 是两套机制

---

## 针对你当前项目的操作建议

1. 确认 Cursor 里已安装 SpecStory 扩展  
2. 续做网站相关工作时，在新对话里 `@` 引用：  
   `2026-05-19_12-50-55Z-website-design-for-beijing-dachuan-technology.md`  
3. 若希望“大川蓝、对角圆角、悬停高亮”等约束自动生效，开启 Derived Rules 或手动写 `.cursor/rules/`

如果你希望，我可以进一步说明：Derived Rules 的具体设置项，或如何写一条 `.mdc` 规则把那次网站设计的关键约束固化下来（Ask 模式下我只能给步骤，不能直接改文件；需要我帮你写规则可切换到 Agent 模式）。

---

## 历史对话简练总结

**主题**：如何让 Cursor 使用 `.specstory` 读取历史对话

**用户问题**：希望 Cursor 能利用 `.specstory` 里的历史对话内容。

**核心结论**：
- `.specstory/history/` 只负责**存档**，Cursor **不会自动**加载全部历史
- 目录名是 `.specstory`（不是 `.spectory`）

**三种让 Cursor 用到历史的方式**：

| 方式 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| `@` 引用 | 在对话中 `@` 指定 `.specstory/history/*.md` 文件（项目已配置 `.cursorindexingignore` 排除索引但允许 `@` 引用） |
| Derived Rules | 开启 `specstory.derivedRules.enabled`，自动提炼规则到 `.cursor/rules/derived-rules.mdc` |
| 手写 Rules | 把长期有效的决策手动写入 `.cursor/rules/*.mdc` |

**项目现状**：
- 已有 SpecStory 历史文件（含网站设计对话）
- 已开启云同步（`specstory.cloudSync.enabled: always`）
- 尚无 `.cursor/rules/` 目录，Derived Rules 可能未启用

**实操建议**：续做网站任务时，用 `@` 引用 `2026-05-19_...-website-design-for-beijing-dachuan-technology.md`；若需长期约束（大川蓝、对角圆角等），开启 Derived Rules 或手写规则文件。

---

## 历史对话简练总结

**项目**：北京大川激流科技有限公司官网（`web-veloxisai`）  
**时长/规模**：单次长会话，约 6000 行，11 轮用户提问

---

### 一、建站（初始需求）

参照 [Porsche 官网](https://www.porsche.com/usa/) 风格，实现静态官网：

| 要求 | 实现 |
|------|------|
| 主题色「大川蓝」 | RGB(6, 126, 249) |
| 业务板块 | CSS Grid 等距矩形布局 |
| 卡片形状 | 对角圆角 `border-radius: 50px 0 50px 0` |
| 悬停交互 | 默认灰度暗淡 → 悬停彩色高亮 + 蓝色渐变 |
| 产出 | `index.html` + `style.css` |

---

### 二、迭代优化（6 次）

1. **Logo** → 换成 `logo-大川激流-大川蓝.png`
2. **Favicon** → 说明如何设置浏览器标签页图标（`favicon.svg` 等）
3. **Hero 背景** → 静态图改视频 `fig/veloxis-dynamic-bg.mp4`（静音、循环、移动端降级为静态图）
4. **标题位置** → Hero 文字移至画面左下角
5. **业务链接** → 「人工智能学习基地」需链到平行文件夹 `web-veloxis-ai-roadmap`（Vite + React 项目）
6. **部署整合** → 域名 `www.veloxisai.com` 给主站，roadmap 作为 `/roadmap/` 子路径

---

### 三、部署方案（后半段重点）

**目标结构**：
```
www.veloxisai.com/          → 大川激流主官网
www.veloxisai.com/roadmap/    → AI 学习基地（React 构建产物）
```

**关键改动**：
- `roadmap/vite.config.ts` 设置 `base: '/roadmap/'`
- React Router 加 `basename="/roadmap/"`
- 主站卡片链接指向 `/roadmap/`
- 新建仓库 `veloxisai-website`，Monorepo 方式（思路 B，手动部署）

**部署步骤概要**（9 步）：
1. 整理目录结构（主站根目录 + `roadmap/` 放构建产物）
2. 添加 `CNAME`（`www.veloxisai.com`）
3. 本地验证
4. 推送到新 GitHub 仓库
5. 从老仓库释放自定义域名
6. 新仓库启用 GitHub Pages 并绑定域名
7. DNS 验证
8. 等待部署生效
9. 逐项验收（首页、视频、卡片跳转、roadmap 路由、HTTPS）

---

### 四、当前状态

- 主站 HTML/CSS 及视觉交互已基本完成
- Hero 视频、Logo、Favicon 已配置
- roadmap 子路径整合方案与代码改造已完成说明
- 用户选择**手动部署**，对话以详细部署指南收尾，尚未确认是否已成功上线
